articles.title: INTRODUCING A NEW DESIGN FOR MICROSOFT 365 COPILOT
articles.summary: Microsoft 365 Copilot has been redesigned to move with how work actually happens — cleaner, faster, and better connected across apps. The updated app, which loads over 50% faster, features a task-aware prompt workspace and a "Work IQ" layer that draws on emails, files, chats, and meetings. Since rolling out the new in-app experiences, Copilot usage rose 27% in Word, 33% in Excel, 43% in PowerPoint, and 30% in Outlook.

articles.title: AUTODESK BUYS MAINTAINX FOR $3.6BN TO PUSH FROM DESIGN INTO OPERATIONS
articles.summary: Autodesk is acquiring MaintainX, a maintenance and operations platform, for $3.6 billion in an all-cash deal. Founded in 2018 and last privately valued at $2.5 billion, MaintainX serves over 500,000 frontline workers with mobile-first maintenance software. The purchase fills the missing "operate" layer in Autodesk's design-to-operations strategy, while also giving it a stream of maintenance data to fuel its AI ambitions.

articles.title: IPHONE 18 PRO DUMMY UNITS REVEAL FOUR COLOR OPTIONS
articles.summary: Leaked iPhone 18 Pro dummy units suggest Apple will offer four colors this year: dark cherry (a deep wine-red), black, silver, and light blue. The lineup appears to drop the popular cosmic orange option from the iPhone 17 Pro, though the colors shown on dummy units may not exactly match Apple's final production models since they are primarily used by accessory manufacturers.

articles.title: DESIGNING FOR AI MEANS DESIGNING LIKE IT'S 1999
articles.summary: The unsettled state of AI design mirrors the early web of 1999 — standards still shifting, no killer app yet, business models unproven. Chat is today's default interface but likely a placeholder, since 95% of generative-AI pilots still deliver no measurable business impact. Designers who thrive will treat this moment as an invitation to invent new conventions, just as their predecessors did in that earlier era.

articles.title: THE PART OF AGILE DESIGNERS FEAR THE MOST: IMPERFECT WORK
articles.summary: Agile's promise of continuous improvement exposes designers' deepest fear — shipping imperfect work — because teams frequently move on without iterating on released features. The word "viable" in MVP is often sidelined by "minimum," yet a poorly designed release yields unreliable feedback — users reject the experience, not the idea. True iteration — cycles of release, observation, and structural refinement — is what makes early releases worth shipping, freeing designers to build responsibly for the present.

articles.title: DESIGNING HOW DESIGNERS MASTER AI
articles.summary: A Salesforce UX team discovered that mastering AI tools is less about learning fixed workflows and more about developing personal ways of working that amplify individual strengths, experiences, and thinking styles. Through six months of peer-led learning sessions, they found that the most valuable knowledge came not from formal training but from colleagues sharing real-world experiments, failures, and workflows, helping others see "a window into someone's thinking" and build their own relationship with AI tools.

articles.title: AI PRESENTATION WORKSPACE
articles.summary: Pitch is an AI presentation workspace where professionals collaborate on, create, and deliver winning slide decks — all while staying on brand.

articles.title: EMOJI AUTOCOMPLETE
articles.summary: Type a colon and search any emoji, symbol, or shortcode in seconds — in TextEdit, iMessage, Terminal, anywhere.

articles.title: TURN PRODUCT VISION INTO ENGINEERING-READY WORK
articles.summary: Go from messy inspiration to shaped, shippable work in moments with Rezonant.

articles.title: FOUR NEW WAYS TO GO FROM IDEA TO PRODUCT WITH AI TOOLS
articles.summary: AI tools are reshaping how product teams at FloQast, Merkle, Affirm, and Accor move from idea to production through four distinct workflows. These include testing in code before designing, exploring layout variations at scale on canvas, prototyping before writing specs, and shipping with design system context intact. Rather than a fixed sequence, successful teams choose their starting point based on the question they need to answer, moving fluidly between tools.

articles.title: EY STUDIO AVOIDED ‘GOURMET' TRENDS FOR SOMETHING MORE AUTHENTIC TO REBRAND ITALY'S MOST ICONIC PANETTONE
articles.summary: Italian design agency Ey Studio rebranded the iconic confectionery brand Alemagna by drawing inspiration from Italy's post-war modernist design era, combining handcrafted geometric typography, archival imagery, and updated packaging to honor the brand's heritage while making it feel contemporary. The studio deliberately avoided both luxury and experimental design trends, aiming instead for an authentic, accessible identity that reflects Alemagna's long-standing connection with everyday Italian culture.

articles.title: APPLE'S NEW SPORTS APP SHOWS US EXACTLY WHAT GOOD DATA VISUALISATION LOOKS LIKE
articles.summary: Apple has expanded its Apple Sports app to more than 170 countries ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, introducing features like a tournament bracket view, visual team formations, Live Activities, and widgets that make following complex competitions easier. The app is a strong example of effective information design. It uses clear hierarchy, visual cues, and simplicity to present large amounts of real-time data without overwhelming users.

articles.title: THIS IS WHAT A THEATER LOOKS LIKE WHEN ARCHITECTURE GETS OUT OF THE WAY
articles.summary: The Samuel H. Scripps Theater Center in Garrison, New York marks Hudson Valley Shakespeare's first permanent home, replacing decades under a seasonal tent.

articles.title: DOES AI FIND REAL UI PROBLEMS OR JUST HALLUCINATIONS?
articles.summary: Two LLMs matched roughly half the usability problems found by human researchers while also flagging 11 issues no human identified.

articles.title: THESE SLICK FIFA WORLD CUP POSTERS ARE WORKS OF ART
articles.summary: Design agency Mucho has created 48 playful, design-led posters inspired by the culture and identity of every nation competing in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, celebrating the intersection of football and graphic design.
